message:
fix(reactor): epoll poll returns count of actually stored events

Previously poll() counted raw epoll events seen, but the Events
container may silently drop entries when its fixed capacity is reached.
The returned count could exceed events.len(), causing callers to
over-iterate or misreport readiness.

Now returns events.len() after the conversion loop so the count always
matches what callers can actually observe. Adds a regression test
confirming that a zero-capacity Events buffer yields count == 0 even
when the kernel delivers readable events.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
